items.reason: All 15 required decision areas are documented in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to. The five new decisions (DEC-011 privacy-surfaces, DEC-012 runtime-coverage-per-spec, DEC-013 audit-response-truth, DEC-014 static-mirror-policy, DEC-015 service-role-policy) are present and each consequences section names work eliminated (e.g., DEC-011 'Removes the need to delete synthetic directory fields...').

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` is referenced multiple times (in DEC-012, lead-spec-accounting, override priority 5) but its actual content is not in the evidence, so I cannot verify each pending entry has owner, target_date, and a one-line description tied to a blocker. The structure exists but substance is unverifiable from evidence.

Every decision DEC-001 through DEC-015 in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` opens its consequences with a 'Removes the need for...' or 'Eliminates...' bullet that names work deleted (e.g., DEC-010 'Removes the need for application code to perform per-table tenant filtering; PostgreSQL RLS is the single runtime gate'; DEC-015 'Removes the need to audit every Edge Function for hidden bypass behavior').

Dictionary fields in `dictionary/*.v1.json` carry `decision_id` on every field and the trace block in `standards-overlap-decisions.md` cross-references them. However, the RLS policies in `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` and `supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json` carry no inline decision references, the Edge Functions under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` have no decision_id comments (only the inline comment 'Admin operation: ...' in tests), and `site/api/*.json` mirrors are not visibly tagged to DEC-014. Override priority 6 explicitly required this work; it is incomplete.

`supabase/functions/audited-roster-read/index.ts` still returns a hard-coded `audit: { logged: 5, fields: [...] }` block without reading back `audit_log` rows under the caller JWT, directly contradicting DEC-013-audit-response-truth which states 'it must not hard-code logged counts or field lists' and 'must read the written audit_log rows back ... before claiming success.' Override priority 4 explicitly flagged this divergence and it remains unfixed.

`docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md` honestly distinguishes OneRoster core (runnable: SQLite, Supabase REST, RLS, live tests) from QTI/CASE (no runtime) and Caliper/LTI/Security/Privacy (partial Edge Function receipt paths only). This matches DEC-012-runtime-coverage-per-spec which separates 'runtime-backed now' from 'doc-only' from 'partial receipt'. The matrix's Caliper row honestly states 'Partial: authenticated Edge Function receipt path' rather than claiming full runtime.

`tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py` creates two tenant-scoped Auth users via service role (allowlisted in `docs/admin-operations.md`) and verifies cross-tenant REST reads are blocked through PostgREST. `tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py` calls the live `audited-roster-read` function then reads `audit_log` directly via REST to confirm rows with client_id, scope, purpose, field_accessed, tenant_id, and timestamp were written. The portal `site/index.html` includes copy-pasteable curls against the real Supabase URL `qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co`.

`site/index.html` has 'Decisions' in primary nav (`Decisions`), but the rendered #decisions section in `site/app.js` hardcodes nine generic marketing-style decisions ('Canonical Data Model', 'SQL Contract', etc.) — NOT the canonical DEC-001 through DEC-015 from `standards-overlap-decisions.md`. None are addressable by their rubric-required anchors like `#dec-001-person-agent-subject`, and consequences/projects_to are not rendered. The footer links to a single rendered MD dump (`docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html`) which the substance_check explicitly rejects.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py` runs at the end of VERIFY.md and writes `site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, but the report dict assembled in `main()` contains only `rubricVersion, evaluatedAt, model, counts, done, items, advisoryScoreGate, runtimePrereqStatus` — there is no `projections` section summarizing decision-to-reality matches. The substance_check explicitly states 'Report missing the projections summary fails (it's the new spine signal).'

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` and `overrides_paused()`, calls them at the start of each iteration BEFORE any work ('if overrides_paused(overrides): ... return 0'), and `build_prompt()` injects the cleaned overrides text into the prompt under 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' via `format_overrides_for_prompt()`. The PAUSE check correctly halts before the next iteration runs.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` terminates with 'if after_eval and after_eval.get("done"): ... return 0' referencing the LLM evaluator's done flag, not a numeric score threshold. Hard caps (max-iterations default 25, stall-limit default 3, cost ceiling) are present as safety rails. The publish gate uses `rubric_progressed()` (counts of pass/partial/fail) rather than score deltas.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
